behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: The risk is module handoff correctness (git-state computation + age threshold + PR-presence join). Behavior text does not require live network, subprocess contracts, or externally visible round-trip output. Higher level rejected: System is disqualified because no explicit real external contract/readback requirement is stated here; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI model decisions are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real staleness detector with real internal modules (state calculator, age policy, PR-state adapter) using deterministic fixtures/stubs for git/PR inputs; verify stale-no-PR is returned only when all three conditions are true.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: This is a bootstrap/runtime emission behavior; false confidence is likely unless tested through the real session-start path and observed output. It requires proving an externally visible side effect (warning text/context appears at startup), which matches System round-trip visibility.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real session/bootstrap entrypoint in a controlled repo state marked stale, then assert the emitted startup output includes the stale-work warning and actionable branch context fields.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly a local deterministic mapping function with no I/O, no cross-module handoff requirement, and no external side effect.
behaviors.test_description: Table-drive the classifier function with all relevant branch-state combinations and assert exact label outputs (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) and determinism.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly requires live repo and PR state plus rendered output, so real external contract/round-trip visibility must be validated end-to-end. Mocks could hide contract or wiring mismatches.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` in a real repo context with real PR-state source and assert rendered output shows `clean` in one scenario and `STALE` in the no-PR scenario.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is state-join/orchestration logic across local modules (branch status + PR lifecycle mapping). Behavior text does not explicitly require live PR API contract checks or external rendered readback. Higher level rejected: System disqualified by lack of explicit live/external contract requirement in this behavior text; Agentic/Workflow disqualified because no AI steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run branch-state evaluation with real internal wiring and PR-state fixtures for `open` and `merged`; assert classifier/result never emits `stale-no-pr` for those states.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real git history transformations (rebase/squash semantics) and readback of resulting branch classification; this is subprocess/OS-backed behavior where fakes often miss edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: Create real branches in a temporary repo, perform actual rebase/squash merge operations, run the detector, and assert post-operation state is not classified as unPRed stale work.
